connections.note: Access via Route29Route46Gate building warp at tile (27,1)

notes: 60 tiles wide x 18 tiles tall (30 blocks x 9 blocks)
West edge connects to Cherrygrove City, east edge to New Bark Town
Gate building at tile (27,1) leads north to Route 46
Ledges along central strip allow one-way shortcuts east toward New Bark Town
Two grass patches: one on western half, one flanking the ledge zone
Two signs: Route29Sign1 at tile (51,7), Route29Sign2 at tile (3,5)
Fruit tree (berry): tile (12,2). Item ball (Potion): tile (48,2)
NPC positions from pokecrystal object_event data (tile coordinates)
Tuscany appears only on Tuesday after player has Zephyr Badge

ROUTE29_COOLTRAINER_M1_DUDE.note: Catching Tutorial NPC (CooltrainerM), tile coords from asm

npcs.name: Catching Tutorial Dude
npcs.dialogue_summary: Offers to demonstrate how to catch Pokemon. Initiates tutorial battle with Lv5 Rattata. Repeatable if player has not yet delivered Mystery Egg to Elm.
npcs.trigger: SCENE_ROUTE29_CATCH_TUTORIAL via coord_events at tiles (53,8) and (53,9)

npcs.name: Youngster
npcs.dialogue_summary: Warns player to keep weak Pokemon out of the tall grass.

npcs.name: Teacher
npcs.dialogue_summary: Explains that ledges are one-way shortcuts — jump south off ledges to reach New Bark Town without walking through the grass.

npcs.name: Fisher
npcs.dialogue_summary: Took a break and saved to record progress.

npcs.name: CooltrainerM
npcs.dialogue_summary: Waiting for Pokemon that appear only at night (says he's waiting for night during day, and for morning during night). Hints at time-based encounters.

npcs.name: Tuscany
npcs.dialogue_summary: Appears only on Tuesdays after player has the Zephyr Badge. Gives the player a Pink Bow (boosts Normal-type moves) as a gift on first Tuesday meeting. Subsequent Tuesdays she talks about her siblings (seven siblings of the week).
npcs.condition: TUESDAY only, after ENGINE_ZEPHYRBADGE flag is set

items.name: Potion
items.note: Item ball on north ledge area, eastern portion of route

signs.text: ROUTE 29 / CHERRYGROVE CITY - NEW BARK TOWN
ROUTE 29 / CHERRYGROVE CITY - NEW BARK TOWN

grass.note: 10% encounter rate each time period, from johto_grass.asm def_grass_wildmons ROUTE_29

water.note: No surfable water on Route 29. No entry in johto_water.asm.

fishing.note: No fishing on Route 29. No bodies of water present.

events.description: Catching Tutorial Dude initiates tutorial when player walks onto coord_event tiles near east end of route. Teaches catching mechanics with a Lv5 Rattata. Scene is set to NOOP once completed or declined.
Tuscany of Tuesday appears on the route (west-central section). Gives Pink Bow on first Tuesday encounter after player has obtained the Zephyr Badge from Violet City.

fruit_trees.note: Berry fruit tree — gives one berry per day. Represented by SPRITE_FRUIT_TREE object.

notes: Route 29 is the first route in the game. It runs east-west connecting New Bark Town to Cherrygrove City. The route features ledges along its central strip that can only be jumped southward, allowing players heading west (to Cherrygrove) to take a shortcut by jumping the ledges. Heading east (to New Bark) requires walking through the tall grass. There are no trainers — this is intentional as it is the first area the player explores before receiving their starter Pokemon. The catching tutorial DudeNPC demonstrates Pokemon catching using a Lv5 Rattata loaded via loadwildmon, not a trainer battle. Route 29 uses TREEMON_SET_ROUTE for Headbutt encounters.
